content_markdown:
A wave travels along a string. Graph M shows the variation with time of the displacement of a point X on the string. Graph N shows the variation with distance of the displacement of the string. PQ and RS are marked on the graphs.

What is the speed of the wave?

diagram_metadata.description: Two sinusoidal wave graphs. Graph M is displacement vs time, showing points P and Q at consecutive peaks. Graph N is displacement vs distance, showing points R and S at consecutive troughs.

vibe_explanation: In Graph M (displacement vs time), the horizontal distance between two consecutive peaks PQ represents the period $T$. In Graph N (displacement vs distance), the horizontal distance between two consecutive troughs RS represents the wavelength $\lambda$. The wave speed $v$ is defined as $v = \frac{\lambda}{T}$. Substituting the variables from the graphs, we get $v = \frac{RS}{PQ}$.
